name: Poolside Central Studio
subtitle: Balcony & Beach Walk
tagline: Perfectly located. The beach is your backyard
welcomeMessage: Compact studio right in the heart of Paphos, steps from the historic port. Full kitchen, sunny balcony, and shared pool, everything you need. Enjoy!

checkin.keyLocation: The key box is located on the left side of the wall as you face the main gate. For more details see photo 3 & photo 4 in the Getting Here section above.
steps: Head to Byzantium Gardens. The complex is right next to Kings Avenue Mall (see photo 1 for map overview)
At the entrance, find the key boxes on the left-hand wall (photo 2)
Tap the chip/proxy on the reader to open the entrance door (photo 3)
Open the left box in the 2nd row using code: 9900 to collect your keys (photo 4)
If arriving by car, use the machine on your right-hand side as you face the main gate to open it (photo 5)
Drive in, pass Block B. Block C and parking are just ahead (photo 6)
At Block C, take the entrance on the left and head up to the 1st floor. Lift available (photo 7)
Your apartment is door No. 106, right next to the elevator (photo 8)
checkin.notes: If you have any trouble finding us, call or WhatsApp anytime, we're happy to guide you step by step.

gettingHere.byCar: Head to Kings Avenue Mall, Byzantium Gardens is right next door. ⚠️ Google Maps may route you through a pedestrian promenade on the last stretch, go around the block instead. See the photo guide below.
gettingHere.parking: Free parking anywhere in the complex. Use the chip on your keychain to open the main gate. To exit by car, pull up to the STOP/WAIT sign, the gate opens automatically.
gettingHere.publicTransport: Bus 612 from Paphos Airport, get off at Kings Avenue Mall (3-min walk to the apartment). Missed it? Next stop is Tombs of the Kings, a 5-min walk. ⚠️ Cash only. €2.50 per ride.
gettingHere.taxi: Taxis wait outside arrivals at Paphos Airport. Fare: €40–45 (day), €45–50 (after hours). ~20–25 min. A slightly cheaper option is to order a Bolt ride.

directionPhotos.caption: Navigate to the Google Maps location. The complex is 2 blocks to the left of Kings Avenue Mall. The red marks show roads to avoid; follow the correct route to reach the front entrance. You will also see a No Entry sign at the entrance. You can disregard it, it applies to non-residents only. As a guest you are allowed to drive in.
Once you reach the entrance, find the key boxes on the left-hand wall.
Use the small plastic chip (proxy) on the keychain. Tap it on this reader to open the entrance door.
Open the left box of the 2nd row (highlighted in red) with the code: 9900 to collect your keys.
Arriving by car? Use this machine to open the main gate. You'll find it on your right-hand side as you face the gate.
Once inside, keep going straight and pass Block B. Block C (your apartment) is just ahead.
At Block C, take the entrance on the left and head to the first floor. There is a lift available.
Look for door No. 106, right next to the elevator.
Exiting by car: pull up in front of the STOP / WAIT sign at the main gate. The doors open automatically.

houseRules: No parties or events. Quiet hours 11pm to 8am
Clean dishes immediately and keep the kitchen counter clear. Cyprus's warm weather can attract insects
Please use the bin next to the toilet. Do not flush toilet paper (Cyprus plumbing cannot handle it)
Shared pool: no glass, no diving, be mindful of other guests
Close balcony door and windows when air conditioning is running
Return keys to the keybox and reset the code at checkout
Treat the apartment with care. Please report any breakages promptly
Keep the keys safe. If you lose them or lock them inside, a locksmith call-out will be needed and the cost will be passed on to you

appliances.name: Electricity
appliances.instructions: Each socket has its own wall switch, if an appliance isn't working, check it's turned on. EU adapters provided.

appliances.name: Kitchen
appliances.instructions: Electric stove, oven, coffee maker and grinder. Salt, pepper, olive oil, and coffee beans provided, help yourself. ⚠️ Clean dishes promptly and keep counters clear, warm weather attracts insects.

appliances.name: Washing Machine
appliances.instructions: In the kitchen. Quick Wash works for most loads.

appliances.name: Shower & Hot Water
appliances.instructions: Takes 2–3 min to warm up, normal. If no hot water, press the heating button next to the bathroom door and wait 20 min. Turn off the heater before stepping in.

appliances.name: Pool Access
appliances.instructions: No key needed, open to all guests. Rules posted by the elevator. Use the provided beach towels, not bathroom towels.

appliances.name: Bathroom
appliances.instructions: Towels, hair dryer, and basic toiletries provided. ⚠️ Use the bin next to the toilet, do not flush toilet paper.

appliances.name: Drinking Water
appliances.instructions: Tap water is safe, but many prefer bottled. Available at nearby supermarkets.

items.name: Faros Beach / Lighthouse Beach
items.tip: One of the closest beach options. Around 15–25 min on foot. Great for sunset, a casual swim, or a coastal walk.

items.name: Municipal Baths, Kato Paphos
items.tip: More of a city swimming spot than a sandy beach, but very practical. Good for a quick dip, promenade walk, or coffee nearby.

items.name: Alykes Beach, Kato Paphos
items.distance: 2–2.5 km, car, bus, or long walk
items.tip: Good if you want sunbeds, restaurants, and an easy beach day close to town. Organised beach, great for families.

items.name: Venus Beach / Tombs of the Kings area
items.distance: 2.5–3 km, car, bus, or long walk
items.tip: Still close by car or bus. Useful if also visiting Tombs of the Kings. Good for a quick swim or sunset.

items.name: Sandy Beach, Chloraka
items.tip: A nice less touristy option, especially when the sea is calm. Local feel, great for sunset and surfers.

items.name: Coral Bay Beach
items.tip: The safest proper sandy beach recommendation near Paphos. Organised and easy. Great for a full beach day, families, and swimming.

items.name: Laourou / Corallia Beach
items.tip: My preferred alternative to Coral Bay. Similar convenience, usually a bit calmer. Quieter sandy beach.

items.name: Sea Caves, Peyia
items.tip: Dramatic coastline. Better for scenery and photos than a classic beach day. Stunning at sunset.

items.name: Agios Georgios Beach, Peyia
items.tip: Nice to combine with Sea Caves, Edro III Shipwreck, or Oniro by the Sea. Good for snorkelling and a relaxed small beach trip.

items.name: White River Beach / Toxeftra
items.tip: Raw and beautiful. Better for exploring than a comfortable sunbed day. A hidden gem with a wild coast feel.

items.name: Petra tou Romiou (Aphrodite's Rock)
items.tip: Beautiful, iconic stop. Better for photos and the mythology experience than swimming. Rocky and can be rough.

items.name: Lara Beach / Lara Bay
items.tip: One of the most special beaches in the Paphos area. Rough access and no proper facilities. Worth it for the wild beach and turtle nesting grounds.

items.name: Latchi Harbour (Blue Lagoon boat trips)
items.tip: Best route if you want Blue Lagoon without rough off-road driving. Book a boat trip from Latchi Harbour.

items.name: Blue Lagoon, Akamas
items.tip: Most impressive water in the wider Paphos area. Not a drive-up beach. Car plus boat is the way to go. Perfect for snorkelling and a full boat day.

items.name: Paphos Coastal Walkway
items.tip: One of the easiest scenic walks from this location. Walk towards Lighthouse Beach, the harbour, and Paphos Castle. Great for sea views and sunset.

items.name: Lighthouse Beach & Lighthouse area
items.tip: A very practical nearby walk with sea views. Good in the morning or before sunset. Scenic walk with a beach stop.

items.name: Paphos Harbour & Paphos Castle
items.tip: Good for an easy evening plan. Combines history, sea views, food, and a relaxed walk. Great harbour views and restaurants nearby.

items.name: Kato Paphos Archaeological Park
items.tip: One of the top cultural sites in Cyprus and very convenient from this location. Roman mosaics and ruins. Best visited in the morning during hot months.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings
items.distance: 2.2–3 km, car, bus, or long walk
items.tip: One of the most important historical sites in Paphos. Worth visiting even if not usually into archaeology. Great sea views and a nice sunset walk.

items.name: Old Town Paphos & Kennedy Square
items.distance: 2–3 km, car, bus, or uphill walk
items.tip: Nice for a cultural walk, cafés, small shops, and a more local Paphos feeling. Best late afternoon.

items.name: Agios Neophytos Monastery
items.tip: A beautiful half-day cultural stop. Quiet, scenic, and more local than the main tourist sites. Stunning frescoes and peaceful views.

items.name: Sea Caves & Edro III Shipwreck viewpoint
items.tip: More scenic than historical, but very guest friendly. Combine with Oniro by the Sea, Sea Caves, and Agios Georgios. Great for photos and sunset.

items.name: Agios Georgios Archaeological Site, Peyia
items.tip: A calmer alternative to the famous Paphos sites. Nice if already driving towards Sea Caves or Lara. Quiet ruins with sea views.

items.name: Petra tou Romiou (Aphrodite's Rock)
items.tip: One of the most iconic scenic stops in Cyprus. Better for views and photos than swimming. Mythology, dramatic coast, unforgettable scenery.

items.name: Akamas Peninsula scenic drive & walking stops
items.distance: 30–45 km depending on stop, car, buggy, or guided tour
items.tip: Best for adventurous guests. The landscape feels much wilder than central Paphos. Road conditions vary. A buggy or guided tour is recommended for the rougher tracks.

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall
items.tip: Very practical and basically next door. Shopping, food court, cinema. Good on very hot, windy, or rainy days.

items.name: Paphos Harbour
items.distance: 1.8–2.3 km, walk, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: One of the easiest evening plans. Good for dinner, drinks, and a relaxed walk by the sea.

items.name: Paphos Castle
items.distance: 2–2.5 km, walk, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: Best combined with Paphos Harbour and the coastal promenade. Great for photos and harbour views.

items.name: Paphos Archaeological Park
items.distance: 1.8–2.5 km, walk, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: One of the top cultural attractions in Paphos. Roman mosaics and ruins. Best in the morning during hot months.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings
items.distance: 2.2–3 km, car, bus, taxi, or long walk
items.tip: A must-see historical site in Paphos and easy to combine with a coastal walk. Ancient history, photos, sea views.

items.name: Old Town Paphos
items.distance: 2–3 km, car, bus, taxi, or uphill walk
items.tip: Nice for a more local Paphos feeling. Cafés, local shops, street art. Best late afternoon or early evening.

items.name: Paphos Aphrodite Waterpark
items.tip: Best family attraction nearby. Easy half or full day option. Great for families and kids on hot days.

items.name: Coral Bay & Corallia Beach
items.tip: The easiest classic beach excursion from central Paphos. Good if guests want sand, sunbeds, and facilities.

items.name: Horse Riding in the Paphos area
items.distance: 10–25 km depending on provider, car or arranged pickup
items.tip: A nice alternative to beach and sightseeing. Great for couples, families, and a nature experience. Best booked in advance.

items.name: Adonis Baths Waterfalls
items.tip: Popular excursion. The road can be rough. Better for adventurous guests. Great for nature, photos, and a waterfall swim.

items.name: Sea Caves & Edro III Shipwreck
items.tip: Very easy scenic excursion from Paphos. Great with a stop at Oniro by the Sea. Dramatic coastline and stunning sunset.

items.name: Paphos Zoo
items.tip: Common family attraction near Coral Bay and Peyia. Easy to combine with Sea Caves or Coral Bay.

items.name: Wine Villages around Kathikas
items.tip: Great for adults who want a more local experience. Wine tasting, local villages, food. Better with a tour if everyone wants to taste wine.

items.name: Avakas Gorge
items.tip: One of the best nature walks in the Paphos region. Wear proper shoes and avoid after heavy rain or extreme heat.

items.name: Akamas Jeep Safari
items.distance: 30–45 km depending on route, 4x4 or buggy tour
items.tip: Best for guests who want an adventure experience without driving difficult roads themselves. Wild nature and great viewpoints.

items.name: Polis & Latchi
items.tip: A calmer side of Cyprus compared with central Paphos. Good for lunch, a coastal walk, and Blue Lagoon boat trip departures.

items.name: Blue Lagoon Boat Trip from Latchi
items.distance: 40–44 km to Latchi, then boat, car plus boat or organised tour
items.tip: One of the best full day excursions from Paphos. Most guests will enjoy this if they like swimming and boat trips. Clear water and snorkelling.

items.name: Baths of Aphrodite
items.tip: Nice to combine with Latchi, Blue Lagoon, and Polis. As a standalone stop it is small, but the mythology and setting are lovely.

items.name: Kourion Archaeological Site
items.tip: Excellent if guests want to explore beyond Paphos. The theatre and coastal views are the highlights. Great for a day trip towards Limassol.

items.name: Troodos Mountains & Villages
items.distance: 75–95 km depending on route, car or organised tour
items.tip: Best full day excursion if guests want a break from the coast and summer heat. Mountain villages, monasteries, cooler weather.

items.name: Limassol Marina & Old Town
items.tip: More urban and polished than Paphos. Best combined with Kourion or Omodos for a full day trip.

items.name: Omodos Village
items.tip: One of the prettiest village excursions from Paphos. Works well as part of a Troodos or wine route day. Traditional village, wine, mountain atmosphere.

items.name: Paphos Coastal Walkway
items.tip: Best no-car option. Walk towards Lighthouse Beach, the harbour, and Paphos Castle. Easy, great for sea views and sunset.

items.name: Lighthouse Beach Coastal Walk
items.tip: Very practical from this location. Good for guests who want nature without a proper hike. Short scenic walk with a sea breeze and sunset views.

items.name: Tombs of the Kings coastal loop
items.tip: Not a pure nature trail, but a lovely easy walk combining open landscape, coastline, and ancient tombs. History plus sea views.

items.name: Sea Caves coastal walk, Peyia
items.tip: One of the easiest scenic nature outings by car. Easy to moderate. Combine with Edro III Shipwreck and Oniro by the Sea. Sea cliffs, photos, great at sunset.

items.name: Avakas Gorge Nature Trail
items.tip: Probably the best nature trail near Paphos. Moderate difficulty. Wear proper shoes and avoid after heavy rain or extreme heat. Dramatic gorge and rock walls.

items.name: Lara Beach & Toxeftra coastal area
items.tip: Beautiful but not polished. Moderate. Better for adventurous guests. Access roads can be rough and turtle nesting areas should be respected.

items.name: Smigies Nature Trail, Neo Chorio
items.tip: Good if guests want Akamas scenery but something less demanding than Aphrodite or Adonis trails. Moderate, forest and Akamas views.

items.name: Aphrodite Nature Trail, Akamas
items.tip: One of the most beautiful hikes in the Paphos region. Moderate to hard. Starts near the Baths of Aphrodite with wide views over Polis, Latchi, and Akamas.

items.name: Adonis Nature Trail, Akamas
items.tip: A good alternative to the Aphrodite Trail. Moderate to hard. Best for guests who want a proper Akamas hike and are comfortable with a more active route.

items.name: Baths of Aphrodite short walk
items.tip: Good for guests who want a gentle nature stop rather than a real hike. Easy. Best combined with Latchi or a Blue Lagoon boat trip.

items.name: Aphrodite's Rock coastal viewpoint walk
items.tip: Not a long hike, but a very scenic easy stop. Good for guests driving towards Limassol or the airport. Photos, mythology, dramatic coast.

items.name: Troodos Nature Trails
items.tip: Best full day hiking option, especially in summer when the coast is too hot. Easy to hard difficulty. Troodos offers real forest trails and cooler air.

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall cafés & food court
items.tip: Not special, but very practical if guests want something easy, fast, or indoors.
items.type: Quick food, coffee, families, heat escape

items.name: The 3 Little Pigs Kebab & Grill House
items.tip: A simple nearby option.

items.name: Tea for Two, Tombs of the Kings Road
items.distance: 1 to 1.5 km, walk, car, or taxi
items.tip: Good for an easy breakfast, brunch, or simple lunch without overthinking it.

items.name: Karlina Restaurant
items.distance: 1.5 to 2 km, walk, car, or taxi
items.tip: Good for a straightforward local meal near Tombs of the Kings Road.
items.type: Greek food, easy dinner, casual meal

items.name: Dias Zeus Greek Restaurant
items.distance: 1.5 to 2 km, walk, car, or taxi
items.tip: Reliable Greek option on Tombs of the Kings Avenue.

items.name: Paphos Harbour restaurants
items.distance: 1.8 to 2.5 km, walk, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: Touristy, but still one of the easiest evening plans from this location. Good for food plus a harbour walk.

items.name: King's Restaurant, Harbour area
items.distance: 2 to 2.5 km, walk, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: Longstanding harbour restaurant with sea and castle views.
items.type: Seafood, harbour views, classic Paphos dinner

items.name: Theo's Restaurant, Harbour area
items.distance: 2 to 2.5 km, walk, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: Popular Mediterranean and seafood option in the harbour area.

items.name: The Moorings Café Restaurant
items.distance: 2 to 2.5 km, walk, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: More about location and easy holiday atmosphere than hidden gem dining.

items.name: Old Town Paphos cafés and restaurants
items.distance: 2 to 3 km, car, bus, taxi, or uphill walk
items.tip: Better than the harbour if guests want something a bit more local and less seafront touristy.

items.name: Pallia Ilektriki Restaurant Café
items.distance: 2 to 3 km, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: Good Old Town choice for Mediterranean, European, and Greek food.
items.type: Old Town dinner, Cypriot food, local feel

items.name: Laona Restaurant
items.distance: 2 to 3 km, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: Strong option for home style Cypriot dishes and a more authentic feel than the harbour.

items.name: Koutourou Ouzeri
items.distance: 2 to 3 km, car, bus, or taxi
items.tip: Good hidden gem style option in Old Town.

items.name: Muse Café Kitchen Bar
items.tip: Best for views over Paphos, especially around sunset.

items.name: Oniro by the Sea
items.tip: One of the best scenic drink or dinner recommendations. Combine with Sea Caves and Edro III Shipwreck.
items.type: Sunset drinks, Sea Caves, special dinner

items.name: Kathikas wine village restaurants
items.distance: 22 to 28 km, car or wine tour
items.tip: Great for adults who want something more authentic than the seafront. Best as a longer lunch or early dinner trip.

items.name: Latchi Harbour tavernas
items.tip: Best combined with a Blue Lagoon boat trip, Baths of Aphrodite, or Polis day trip.
items.type: Fish, harbour lunch, Blue Lagoon day

items.name: Kings Avenue Mall
items.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

items.name: Paphos Market
items.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

items.name: Old Town
items.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

shopping.name: Kings Avenue Mall
shopping.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

shopping.name: Paphos Market
shopping.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

shopping.name: Old Town
shopping.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

gettingAround.name: Bus
gettingAround.info: Central bus station (Tombs of the Kings) is a 10-min walk (~700 m) away, with direct connections to main attractions, the airport, and nearby towns.

gettingAround.name: RideNow (Car Sharing)
gettingAround.info: Car-sharing app that works well around Paphos. Sign up in minutes. Cars typically within a 5–10 min walk.

gettingAround.name: Bolt
gettingAround.info: Budget-friendly ride app. Good alternative to metered taxis around Paphos.

gettingAround.name: Taxi
gettingAround.info: Various taxi services are available online. One reliable option is Paphos Taxi Airport, where you can also arrange a pickup directly via WhatsApp: +357 99 460 829.

contacts.name: Emergency Services
contacts.note: Police, Ambulance, Fire. Free from any phone

contacts.name: Paphos District Hospital
contacts.note: Main hospital in Paphos, approx. 10 min drive

emergency.nearestHospital: Paphos District Hospital, approx. 10 min drive.
emergency.nearestPharmacy: Several pharmacies within walking distance on the main road. Ask us if you need a recommendation.

steps: Remove all items from the fridge and dispose of leftover food
Strip the bed and leave linens folded at the foot of the bed
Wash any dishes used and leave them drying on the rack
Empty the rubbish bin and take the bag to the wheelie bins in the car park
Close all windows and the balcony door
Switch off the air conditioning, all lights, and the TV
Return the key to the key box and reset the code
WhatsApp us to let us know you've left. Thanks so much for staying!
checkout.lateCheckout: Late checkout is generally not available due to our tight housekeeping schedule. Please let us know if you have a special circumstance and we'll try our best.
